0ba89a77e4ff8c48: [{"severity":"HIGH","confidence":9,"path":"src/session.ts","line":16,"category":"async-correctness","summary":"store.flush() is called without await, so close() resolves before the flush completes and any rejection from flush() becomes an unhandled promise rejection.","fix":"Restore await before this.store.flush(this.id) so flush completes before persist() runs and rejections are propagated normally."}]
45e614c5c0553abb: [{"severity": "HIGH", "confidence": 7, "path": "src/session.ts", "line": 17, "category": "red-team", "summary": "The orphaned flush() promise continues executing after close() resolves and this.active is set to false, so any caller that tears down or reuses the store after awaiting close() will have an in-flight operation racing against teardown or a subsequent session's state.", "fix": "Await flush() before persist() so all async work tied to this session's lifetime completes before close() resolves and the store is safe to dispose or reassign."}]
78b04acd62f4d875: [{"severity": "CRITICAL", "confidence": 10, "path": "src/session.ts", "line": 15, "category": "api-surface", "summary": "close() now resolves before flush() completes, breaking the contract that all data is durably persisted when the returned Promise resolves.", "fix": "Restore 'await this.store.flush(this.id)' so callers can rely on close() settling only after both flush and persist have completed."}]
